evals.prompt: refactor the api to work with sqlalchemy
evals.expected_output: The YOLO API data layer is refactored from raw sqlite3 to SQLAlchemy. models.py and db.py are created. Raw SQL is replaced with ORM queries. Tests are updated to use dependency_overrides[get_db].
assertions: models.py exists and defines PredictionSession and DetectionObject as SQLAlchemy classes
db.py exists and defines get_db(), SessionLocal, and create_engine
app.py does not contain import sqlite3
app.py does not contain DB_PATH
app.py does not contain init_db
app.py does not contain raw SQL strings like CREATE TABLE, INSERT INTO, SELECT, or DELETE
Every endpoint that reads or writes to the database uses Depends(get_db)
requirements.txt includes sqlalchemy
tests/test_api.py uses app.dependency_overrides[get_db]
tests/test_api.py does not contain DB_PATH or init_db
Running pytest tests/ manually shows 0 failed

evals.prompt: make the database backend configurable so we can use postgres in production
evals.expected_output: db.py reads DB_BACKEND from environment. SQLite is the default. PostgreSQL is used when DB_BACKEND=postgres using DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME.
assertions: db.py reads DB_BACKEND from environment variables
db.py uses sqlite:/// as the default URL when DB_BACKEND is not set
db.py builds a postgresql:// URL when DB_BACKEND is postgres
db.py reads DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_HOST, DB_PORT, and DB_NAME from environment variables
The SQLite engine uses check_same_thread=False
The PostgreSQL engine does not use check_same_thread
requirements.txt includes psycopg2-binary
Running python -c 'import app' does not raise any errors

evals.prompt: add an endpoint GET /predictions/recent that returns the 10 most recent sessions
evals.expected_output: GET /predictions/recent is added to app.py using SQLAlchemy. Returns at most 10 sessions ordered by timestamp descending. Tests verify ordering and limit.
assertions: app.py defines a GET /predictions/recent route
The route uses Depends(get_db)
The query orders results by timestamp descending
The query limits results to 10
The endpoint does not contain raw SQL strings
A test verifies the endpoint returns at most 10 sessions
A test verifies the newest session appears first in the response

UserFeedback has a rating column
No raw CREATE TABLE SQL exists anywhere in models.py or db.py

The feedback test asserts response.status_code before checking the database

Deletion uses SQLAlchemy ORM, not raw DELETE SQL
DetectionObject rows are removed either through cascade on the relationship or explicit ORM deletion
A missing uid returns 404
A test seeds a session with detection objects, calls the delete endpoint, and checks the session row is gone
A test checks that detection objects are also removed after deletion
